title: The Problem
story_beat: Pink Bear wakes up excited for Easter, only to find a thick blanket of snow covering the garden, threatening to ruin the egg hunt.

techniques.name: visual_thinking
techniques.reason: Chris already excels at visualizing scenes, so we'll directly apply this strength to showing the snowy problem.

techniques.name: show_dont_tell
techniques.reason: A brief reminder will help Chris describe the snowy disappointment through actions and observations rather than stating feelings.

techniques.name: character_voice
techniques.reason: This session introduces the problem, and Pink Bear's unique reaction to it is crucial. A full lesson will help Chris develop Pink Bear's distinct voice in expressing disappointment and determination.

wonder_prompt: Imagine your favorite character in a story. How do you know exactly how they feel or what they're thinking, even if the author never says 'she was sad'? What tiny details or words make their personality shine through?
wonder_example: Hey! I'm the Pigeon! I'm not gonna let you drive the bus! I mean, come on, I'm a pigeon! I'm practically a bus driver already. I've got my own wings, I can fly, I'm responsible! (Mo Willems, Don't Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus!)
build_instructions: This session, focus on showing Pink Bear's initial excitement and then the crushing disappointment as she discovers the snowy obstacle to her Easter plans.
page_prompts: Write the moment Pink Bear first wakes up on Easter morning, full of anticipation. What does she see or hear that hints at something unusual outside her window?
Pink Bear ventures closer to the window or door, trying to get a better look at the garden. Describe her first real sight of the heavy snow and her immediate reaction.
The realization sinks in for Pink Bear: the snow is too deep, the garden is buried, and the Easter egg hunt is impossible. Show her deep disappointment without saying 'she was sad.'
reflect_preview: Pink Bear's heart sinks as she stares at the snow-covered garden, her Easter plans a frosty mess. What will she do now to bring Easter joy back to Susie?
